text: The robotic arms of the da Vinci system are positioned and docked. The surgeon begins the procedure by detaching the colopiploid ligament and opening the lassa sac.
The surgeon completes the dissection of the splenocolic ligament, resulting in the mobilization of the splenic flexure.
The surgeon separates the descending mesocolon from Toldt's fascia, with the inferior mesenteric vein visibly divided at its origin.
The surgeon divides the left colonic artery at its root, ensuring preservation of the origins of the inferior mesenteric artery and the sigmoid artery.
The surgeon uses a mechanical stapler to dissect the descending colon, separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon uses a mechanical stapler to dissect the transverse colon, separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon mobilizes the descending colon after opening the left paracolic gutter, completing the separation from surrounding structures.
The surgeon performs an intracorporeal side-to-side isoperistaltic anastomosis, connecting two sections of the colon with aligned peristaltic directions.
The surgeon closes the enterotomy using a double-layer barb-running suture technique. The surgical specimen is extracted through a mini-phanacin incision.
